'''Oliver Ashall-Bott''' (born 24 November 1997) is an English professional [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|fb}} or {{rlp|wg}}er for [[Toulouse Olympique]] in the [[Super League]].

He previously played for the [[Huddersfield Giants]], [[Wakefield Trinity]] and [[Widnes Vikings]] in the [[Super League]] and the [[RFL Championship|Championship]], and on loan from [[Widnes Vikings|Widnes]] at the [[North Wales Crusaders]] in [[RFL League One|League 1]]. Ashall-Bott also played for the [[London Broncos]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]] and spent time away from the [[London Broncos|Broncos]] at the [[Salford Red Devils]] in the [[Super League]]. He spent time on loan from [[Huddersfield Giants|Huddersfield]] at [[Wakefield Trinity]] in [[Super League XXVI]].

==Personal life==
Ashall-Bott was born as Oliver Ashall in [[Widnes]], [[Cheshire]], England. 

He was previously in a relationship with [[England women's national football team|England]] and [[FC Bayern Munich (women)|Bayern Munich]] footballer [[Georgia Stanway]].<ref>{{cite web | url=https://www.hulldailymail.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/toulouse-olympique-squad-hull-kr-7410857 | title=Toulouse boosted by key returnee as Sylvain Houles names squad for Hull KR trip | date=2 August 2022 }}</ref>

==Career==
===Widnes Vikings===
He made his début for the [[Widnes Vikings]] against [[Castleford Tigers|Castleford]] in 2018.<ref name="Widnes Vikings v Castleford Tigers">{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league|title=Widnes Vikings v Castleford Tigers|publisher=BBC|date=11 February 2018|access-date=11 February 2018}}</ref> He went on to play 6 more games scoring one try (Hull KR) and heading on loan to North Wales Crusaders scoring 3 tries in three games. He played 7 games in the 2019 championship season scoring once.

===London Broncos===
On 25 September 2019, it was announced that Ashall-Bott had joined the [[London Broncos]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/49828636|title=Olly Ashall-Bott: London Broncos sign Widnes Vikings full-back|website=BBC Sport|access-date=18 Dec 2020}}</ref>

On 25 September 2020, Ashall-Bott agreed a temporary loan deal with the [[Salford Red Devils]].<ref>{{cite news|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/54301718|title=Salford Red Devils: Tom Gilmore & Olly Ashall-Bott join on loan|work=BBC Sport |access-date=18 Dec 2020}}</ref>

===Huddersfield Giants===
On 25 January 2021, it was reported that he had signed for the [[Huddersfield Giants]] in the [[Super League]].<ref>{{cite web|website=BBC Sport|date=25 Jan 2021|access-date=25 Jan 2021|title=Olly Ashall-Bott: Huddersfield Giants sign ex-London Broncos full-back|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/55794969}}</ref>

===Wakefield Trinity (loan)===
On 26 April 2021, it was reported that he had signed for [[Wakefield Trinity]] in the [[Super League]] on a short-term two-week long loan.<ref>{{cite web|website=Love Rugby League|date=26 Apr 2021|accessdate=26 Apr 2021|title=Wakefield sign Olly Ashall-Bott on loan from Huddersfield|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/wakefield-sign-olly-ashall-bott-on-loan-from-huddersfield/}}</ref><ref>[https://www.yorkshireeveningpost.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/wakefield-trinity-extend-st-helens-hooker-josh-eaves-loan-spell-but-olly-ashall-bott-returns-to-huddersfield-giants-3250097 Wakefield Trinity extend St Helens hooker Josh Eaves' loan spell - but Olly Ashall-Bott returns to Huddersfield Giants]</ref>

===Toulouse Olympique===
In February 2022, it was announced that Ashall-Bott had signed for Toulouse Olympique. Ashall-Bott played 25 games for Toulouse in the 2022 Super League season as they finished last on the table and were relegated back to the RFL Championship.<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.to13.com/actualites/breaking-le-to-signe-olly-ashall-bott/|title=BREAKING : Le TO signe Olly ASHALL-BOTT|website=www.to13.com|date=23 February 2022|language=French}}</ref>
In the 2025 RFL Championship season, he scored 23 tries in 22 games as the club won the RFL Championship grand final defeating [[York RLFC|York]] 10-8.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.yorkpress.co.uk/sport/25522209.sylvain-houles-proud-toulouse-beat-york-knights-grand-final/|title=Sylvain Houles 'proud' as Toulouse beat York Knights in Grand Final|website=www.yorkpress.co.uk}}</ref>
